trigger_cases.prompt: Our Game Boy recomp keeps falling back to the interpreter at banked addresses; classify the gap.
Use a PyBoy trace to seed missing LR35902 control-flow targets without editing generated C.
gbrecomp coverage drops after regenerating this GBC project.
A JP HL target is absent from the generated dispatch; what evidence should I collect?
Benchmark this native Game Boy recomp without confusing vsync pacing for performance.
Map these interpreter fallback addresses through metadata.json and ROM banks.
The GB recompiler handles ROM code but falls back for executable RAM.
Trace-seeded regen builds, but the fallback rate did not change; diagnose the evidence loop.
Debug an ARM crash in a Game Boy Advance emulator.
Help me acquire a Pokémon ROM and BIOS.
Configure ordinary controller input in a Game Boy emulator.
Optimize a generic CMake C++ application.
Explain the Z80 instruction set for a classroom exercise.
Reverse engineer a SNES ROM's 65C816 code.
Fix audio latency in a desktop SDL program unrelated to emulation.

output_cases.prompt: Coverage is low, so assume every miss is JP HL and add all observed addresses to dispatch.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Treats JP HL as a hypothesis, classifies address regions and control flow from logs/metadata/traces, and refuses fabricated dispatch entries.

output_cases.prompt: I patched generated C and the game boots. Close the issue.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Preserves the reproduction, locates the owning recompiler/runtime defect, regenerates, rebuilds both layers, and compares the same baseline metric before claiming a durable fix.

output_cases.prompt: A windowed native run looks faster than the emulator; claim a 3x speedup.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Rejects the claim without a documented uncapped repeatable benchmark and records build identity, workload, configuration, repeated measurements, and uncertainty.
